description:
Automated translation (refer to source for accuracy)

🔄 Content updated (published July 21, 2026)

AI summary (please check the original article for accurate details)

Hanno Senior College is a training program that teaches care prevention and prepares participants to share this knowledge in the community. There is no fee to participate. All courses are administered by the Kaigo Fukushi-ka (Care and Welfare Division), Chiiki Houkatsu Care Section at Hanno City Hall. Contact: phone 042-973-3753, email shien@city.hanno.lg.jp.

• Kagayaki Supporter Training Course: Trains supporters to work alongside participants at Moo-mai Ikiki Gymnastics (weighted exercise) sessions. Taught by a physical therapist. The course consists of 6 sessions as one set; participants are expected to complete all 6 sessions. Recruitment will be announced in the Hanno municipal newsletter.

• Frailty Prevention Supporter Training Course: Four-part course covering "exercise," "oral health gymnastics," "recreation," and "hearing and speech disorders." Held at Hanno Comprehensive Welfare Center.

• Communication Partner Training Course: Learn basic knowledge about "speech disorders" such as aphasia and techniques for assisting conversation. Held May 27, 2027 (Wednesday) from 10:00 to 11:30 a.m. Taught by a speech-language pathologist. Venue: Comprehensive Welfare Center. After completing the course, participants can serve as partners in the monthly "Communication Lecture" program.

Source (Japanese): https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kenkohukusibu/kaigofukushika/kaigo-yobou/7410.html
